$comment: Allowlist for scripts/check-class-policy-boundary.ts. Each entry waives one rule for one file path with a justification. Bypassing a rule is a stop-the-world signal in code review; entries here are the documented exceptions.

entries.reason: Cross-field validator infrastructure legitimately returns ValidationResult unions (the boundary type itself). RULE-1 targets the boundary's clients, not the boundary.

evaluateAccessPolicy() returns 'allow' | 'deny' as part of v7.2.0's policy-evaluation surface (predates ADR-010). Substrate utility per ADR-008; consumers compose it. Migration to a constraint-driven shape tracked for v9.
evaluateEconomicBoundary() returns AccessDecision { granted: bool } shape. Substrate per ADR-008 — the AccessDecision shape is a contract, not a verifier output.
Pre-v8.5.0 (v8.4.0) hash domain. NFC normalization NOT applied (consistent with shipped consumer hashes). Migration to safeCanonicalize would change shipped hashes — see SDD R15.
Pre-v8.5.0 (v7.11.0) scoring-path hash domain. Same legacy-hash-stability rationale as signature.ts.
Pre-v8.5.0 reputation replay hash domain. Same legacy-hash-stability rationale as signature.ts.
Pre-v8.5.0 audit-trail hash domain (v8.0.0). Same legacy-hash-stability rationale as signature.ts.
Synthetic-violation fixtures. The test asserts the lint flags template-literal strings that look like real canonicalize imports — those strings would otherwise self-trigger the rule.

Synthetic-violation fixtures. The test asserts the lint flags template-literal strings that look like real assertValid(SignatureEnvelopeSchema, ...) call sites — those strings would otherwise self-trigger the rule.
